zh.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Fowke是Sir Henry Fowke和Mirabell Harrison的兒子，於1340年出生在英國斯塔福爾布霍德。他和Juliana Fane至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Fowke是Sir Henry Fowke和Mirabell Harrison的儿子，于1340年出生在英国斯塔福爾布霍德。他和Juliana Fane至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Fowke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from the Middle English, Anglo-Norman French personal name Folk, Fulk, Fuke. Compare Foxon . For a variant form of the personal name see Foulkes . See also Fudge , which may sometimes be a variant form.

variants.name: Fudge
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English, Anglo-Norman French personal name Fulch or Fuche, usually a pet form of Old French Fulcher, Fucher (see Fulcher ). Voicing of ‘ch’ to soft ‘g’ gave rise to Fudge. The name may sometimes have been interchangeable with Fowke (for which see Foulk 1), derived from a pet form of this and other Old French personal names in Fulc-.
English: habitational name from Fuge, in Blackawton, Devon. This placename is composed of Old English feoh ‘cattle’ + wīc ‘farm, dairy farm, village’.

variants.name: Folke
meanings: Norman, English:  see  Fowke  .

variants.name: Fook
meanings: Norman, English:  see  Fowke  .

variants.name: Foulk
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English and Anglo-Norman French personal name Fulk, which is of ancient Germanic origin, ultimately from an element meaning ‘folk, people’.

variants.name: Fogg
meanings: English (northern): possibly a variant of Fowke (see Foulk 1). It may derive from Middle English fogge ‘aftermath’ (i.e. grass left to grow after the hay has been cut, also applied to long grass in a water meadow), especially in northern England, where the term occurs as a specifying element in minor placenames, but as it is not found on its own as a simplex name, the derivation is doubtful without some supporting evidence. The word fogge, which is probably of Old Norse origin, is still in use as a dialect term in Craven, Yorkshire, and in eastern Lancashire. modern English fog ‘thick mist’ is first attested in the 16th century and is unlikely to be the source of the surname.

variants.name: Fuke
meanings: Norman, English:  see  Fowke   and  compare  Fook  .

variants.name: Folk
meanings: German: variant of   Volk   1 and, in North America, also an altered form of this. This form of the surname is also found in Czechia.
English: variant of   Foulk   1.

variants.name: Focke
meanings: North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from the ancient Germanic personal name Folco, a pet form of various names beginning with Folc- or Volk-; cognate with Foulk 1.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Foulk   1. See also   Foulks  .

variants.name: Foulke
meanings: English (Derbyshire and Shropshire): variant of   Foulk  .

variants.name: Fulk
meanings: English: variant of   Foulk   1.
Altered form of German   Volk   or   Falk  .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eduardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard
meanings: form of English Edward .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Fowke was born in 1340, in Brewood, Staffordshire, England as the son of Sir Henry Fowke and Mirabell Harrison. He had at least 1 son with Juliana Fane.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Fowke est né en 1340, à Brewood, Staffordshire, Angleterre, fils de Sir Henry Fowke et Mirabell Harrison. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Juliana Fane.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Fowke wurde 1340 in Brewood, Staffordshire, England, als Sohn von Sir Henry Fowke und Mirabell Harrison geboren. Er hatte mit Juliana Fane mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Fowke è nato il nel 1340 nel Brewood, Staffordshire, England come figlio di Sir Henry Fowke e Mirabell Harrison. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Juliana Fane.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Fowke родился в 1340 г. в местности брюуд, стаффордшир, Англия. Его родителей звали Sir Henry Fowke и Mirabell Harrison. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Juliana Fane.
